UAObjectType.Description: A WSAlarmEvent is triggered when an impermissible deviation from the current state of a machine/unit is detected, which leads to a stop of the machine/unit. Alarm messages are first value messages. The "surge of subsequent alarm messages" is not transmitted.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:EncodedMessage: The EncodedMessage is an array of strings containing keys for language-independent text blocks. For example, the key “locationMI3” stands for “Machine inlet:” and the key ‘A4367’ stands for “Voltage protection on the inlet screw.” This mechanism allows a client to translate the respective text blocks into different languages using a dictionary.

UAVariable.Description: The EncodedMessage is an array of strings containing keys for language-independent text blocks. For example, the key “locationMI3” stands for “Machine inlet:” and the key ‘A4367’ stands for “Voltage protection on the inlet screw.” This mechanism allows a client to translate the respective text blocks into different languages using a dictionary.

UAObjectType.Description: A WSWarningEvent is triggered when an impermissible deviation of the current state from the target state of a machine/unit is detected, which did not lead to a standstill of the machine/unit and only the operator was informed. Warning messages are last value messages, so every new warning is transmitted.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:EncodedMessage: The EncodedMessage is an array of strings containing keys for language-independent text blocks. For example, the key “locationMI3” stands for “Machine inlet:” and the key ‘A4367’ stands for “Voltage protection on the inlet screw.” This mechanism allows a client to translate the respective text blocks into different languages using a dictionary.

UAVariable.Description: The EncodedMessage is an array of strings containing keys for language-independent text blocks. For example, the key “locationMI3” stands for “Machine inlet:” and the key ‘A4367’ stands for “Voltage protection on the inlet screw.” This mechanism allows a client to translate the respective text blocks into different languages using a dictionary.

UAObjectType.Description: After transferring or dosing a material into e.g. a tank, a TransferEvent is generated, which contains information such as source, destination, set and actual quantities about the tranfer process.

UAWSBasis:TargetBatchId: Provides information about the target batch id.
UAWSBasis:SourceAssetId: Provides information about the source asset id.
UAWSBasis:TargetAssetId: Provides information about the target asset id.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about a single form, such as the Id of the form.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Id: This data point gives the identification of a single mould or of a single form.
UAWSBasis:Cavities: This property gives the number of cavities resp. products per mould or form.
UAWSBasis:TotalCycles: This data point gives the total number of cycles.
UAWSBasis:InUse: This data point indicates whether a material or form is in use (true) or not (false).

UAVariable.Description: This data point gives the identification of a single mould or of a single form.
This property gives the number of cavities resp. products per mould or form.
This data point gives the total number of cycles.
This data point indicates whether a material or form is in use (true) or not (false).

UAObjectType.Description: This ObjectType is a subtype of the WSFormType and contains information about a single mould for a moulding plant.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:TooWet: This data point indicates if the mould is too wet.
UAWSBasis:TooCold: This data point indicates if the mould is too cold.
UAWSBasis:Temperature: This data point gives the actual temperature.

This data point indicates if the mould is too cold.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about a set of forms.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:FormSetId: This data point gives the identification of a form set, e.g. for the moulds for a moulding plant.
UAWSBasis:FormSetName: This data point gives the name of a form set, e.g. for the moulds for a moulding plant.
UAWSBasis:Cavities: This property gives the number of cavities resp. products per mould or form.
UAWSBasis:<Mould>: Provides information about a single mould of a form set for a moulding plant.
UAWSBasis:<Form>: Provides information about a single form.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about a material used for an article of an order or a batch.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:InUse: This data point indicates whether a material or form is in use (true) or not (false).
UAWSBasis:MaterialId: This data point informs about the code of the currently processed material. These can be raw materials, auxiliaries and other resources.
UAWSBasis:MaterialName: This data point informs about the name of the currently processed material.
UAWSBasis:MaterialRatio: This data point indicates the ratio of this material to the total quantity of all materials. The value is in the range of 0 - 100 % or 0 - 1 .
UAWSBasis:SetQuantity: This data point gives the set quantity of product or material.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Starved: The machine / unit must not produce any products in this state due to an insufficient supply detected at the machine inlet.
UAWSBasis:StarvedBranchline: The machine / unit must not produce any products in this state due to an insufficient supply detected at the machine inlet in the branch line.

UAWSBasis:Prepared: The machine is ready to carry out its intended function. However, it is in a waiting state which was not recognized as a starved or blocked state and can automatically start again.
UAWSBasis:Blocked: The machine must not produce any products in this state because a backlog has been detected at its outlet.
UAWSBasis:BlockedBranchline: The machine must not produce any products in this state because a backlog has been detected at its outlet in the branche line.
UAWSBasis:StarvedToPrepared: Provides information about the starved to prepared.
UAWSBasis:PreparedToStarved: Provides information about the prepared to starved.
UAWSBasis:StarvedBranchlineToPrepared: Provides information about the starved branchline to prepared.
UAWSBasis:PreparedToStarvedBranchline: Provides information about the prepared to starved branchline.
UAWSBasis:BlockedToPrepared: Provides information about the blocked to prepared.
UAWSBasis:PrepredToBlocked: Provides information about the prepred to blocked.
UAWSBasis:BlockedBranchlineToPrepared: Provides information about the blocked branchline to prepared.
UAWSBasis:PreparedToBlockedBranchline: Provides information about the prepared to blocked branchline.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Sterilize: The machine / unit is running the sterilize program. This program can consist of program steps which can be controlled independently of each another.
UAWSBasis:Cleaning: The machine / unit is running the cleaning program. This program can consist of program steps which can be controlled independently of each another.
UAWSBasis:Changeover: The machine / unit is running the changeover program in which automatic machine adjustments are made depending on specific parameters.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:StartUp: Although the machine / unit is functioning as designed by the manufacturer, it is running a start up program which ensures full production after a warm-up period as stipulated by regulations or for safety considerations, or in conjunction with container buffering machines.
UAWSBasis:Production: The machine /unit is functioning as designed by the manufacturer.

UAWSBasis:RunDown: Although the machine / unit is functioning as designed by the manufacturer, it is running a stop program which ensures production stop after a run down period as stipulated by regulations or for safety considerations, or in conjunction with container buffering machines.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about the operation mode none state machine type.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Used: The asset just finished production and is waiting for cleaning arrangements. A reusing of an already used equipment is only possible with manual switching and approval of a responsible operator.
UAWSBasis:Cleaned: The asset just finished the cleaning procedure. This state is valid for a maximum time, before this state changes to “Cleaned Expired”.
UAWSBasis:CleanedExpired: The asset remained in the “Cleaned” state too long and must be rinsed or cleaned again before reuse.
UAWSBasis:Sterile: The asset just finished the Sterilization procedure. This state is valid for a maximum time, before this state changes to “Sterile Expired”.
UAWSBasis:SterileExpired: The asset remained in the “Sterile” state too long and must be sterilized or cleaned again before reuse.

UAObject.Description: The asset just finished production and is waiting for cleaning arrangements. A reusing of an already used equipment is only possible with manual switching and approval of a responsible operator.
The asset just finished the cleaning procedure. This state is valid for a maximum time, before this state changes to “Cleaned Expired”.
The asset remained in the “Cleaned” state too long and must be rinsed or cleaned again before reuse.
The asset just finished the Sterilization procedure. This state is valid for a maximum time, before this state changes to “Sterile Expired”.
The asset remained in the “Sterile” state too long and must be sterilized or cleaned again before reuse.

UAObjectType.Description: The operating mode acording the DIN IEC 60050-351 provides information about the nature and extent of the intervention on the control equipment by the operators, and also via feedback from the equipment (DIN IEC 60050-351).

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Automatic: An operating mode in which the control unit operates without intervention by the operator following a set of control procedures (DINIEC 60050-351). In the context of the Weihenstephan Standards this term means that the machines of a production plant are integrated into a control concept for the entire system and the set output is automatically controlled.

UAWSBasis:SemiAutomatic: An operating mode in which only some of the controls or part of the program operate without intervention by the operator (DIN IEC 60050-351). In the context of the Weihenstephan Standards this term means that the machines of a production plant are not integrated into a control concept for the entire system and the set output is manually controlled on site.

UAWSBasis:Manual: An operating mode in which the control units only operate with intervention by the operator and involve possible locking mechanisms (DIN IEC 60050-351). As opposed to the DIN standard, in the context of the Weihenstephan Standards this term also includes the setup mode, the step setting mode and tipping mode.
UAWSBasis:AutomaticToAutomatic: Provides information about the automatic to automatic.
UAWSBasis:SemiAutomaticToSemiAutomaic: Provides information about the semi automatic to semi automaic.
UAWSBasis:ManualToManual: Provides information about the manual to manual.
UAWSBasis:AutomaticToSemiAutomatic: Provides information about the automatic to semi automatic.
UAWSBasis:SemiAutomaticToAutomatic: Provides information about the semi automatic to automatic.
UAWSBasis:AutomaticToManual: Provides information about the automatic to manual.
UAWSBasis:ManualToAutomatic: Provides information about the manual to automatic.
UAWSBasis:SemiAutomaticToManual: Provides information about the semi automatic to manual.
UAWSBasis:ManualToSemiAutomatic: Provides information about the manual to semi automatic.

UAObject.Description: An operating mode in which the control unit operates without intervention by the operator following a set of control procedures (DINIEC 60050-351). In the context of the Weihenstephan Standards this term means that the machines of a production plant are integrated into a control concept for the entire system and the set output is automatically controlled.

An operating mode in which only some of the controls or part of the program operate without intervention by the operator (DIN IEC 60050-351). In the context of the Weihenstephan Standards this term means that the machines of a production plant are not integrated into a control concept for the entire system and the set output is manually controlled on site.

An operating mode in which the control units only operate with intervention by the operator and involve possible locking mechanisms (DIN IEC 60050-351). As opposed to the DIN standard, in the context of the Weihenstephan Standards this term also includes the setup mode, the step setting mode and tipping mode.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSAssetMachineryBuildingBlocksType refers to all building blocks defined in the CS series OPC UA Machinery (OPC 40001) that are used by the Weihenstephan Standards.

The WSAssetIdentificationType provides globally unique identification of a WSAsset and additional identification information about a WSMachine, WSUnit, and WSComponent. The WSAssetIdentificationType is based on the MachineIdentificationType of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UADI:AssetId: AssetId is a user writable alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a component. The ID is provided by the integrator or user of the device. It contains typically an identifier in a branch, use case or user specific naming scheme. This could be for example a reference to an electric scheme.

UAVariable.Description: AssetId is a user writable alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a component. The ID is provided by the integrator or user of the device. It contains typically an identifier in a branch, use case or user specific naming scheme. This could be for example a reference to an electric scheme.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSAssetMonitoringConsumptionType serves as an entry point for consumption information for a WSAsset and contains information in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSAssetMonitoringHealthType provides health information for a WSAsset in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSAssetMonitoringProcessType provides process information for a WSAsset in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSAssetMonitoringStatusType provides status information to a WSAsset according to the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSAssetMonitoringType provides entry points for monitoring a WSAsset according to the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAMachinery:Consumption: Entry point for consumption information for a WSAsset and is based on the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
UAMachinery:Health: Entry point for health information for the WSAsset.
UAMachinery:Process: Entry point for process information of a WSAsset.
UAMachinery:Status: Entry point for status information of a WSAsset.

UAObject.Description: Entry point for consumption information for a WSAsset and is based on the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).

UAObjectType.Description: The NotificationsType provides notifications to a WSAsset according to the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSAssetType defines the way in which information is structured in a WSMachine, a WSUnit, and a WSComponente.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UADI:Identification: The building block provides globally unique identification of a WSAsset.
UAMachinery:Monitoring: This building block provides entry points for monitoring a WSAsset.
UAMachinery:Notifications: This building block provides the entry point into notifications of a WSAsset.
UAMachinery:MachineryBuildingBlocks: This building block refers to all Machinery building blocks used that are defined in the CS series OPC UA Machinery (OPC 40001) and are used by a WSAsset.

This building block provides entry points for monitoring a WSAsset.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about the w s machine machinery building blocks type.
The WSMachineIdentificationType provides globally unique identification of a WSMachine and additional identification information about a WSMachine.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UADI:AssetId: AssetId is a user writable alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a component. The ID is provided by the integrator or user of the device. It contains typically an identifier in a branch, use case or user specific naming scheme. This could be for example a reference to an electric scheme.

UAVariable.Description: AssetId is a user writable alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a component. The ID is provided by the integrator or user of the device. It contains typically an identifier in a branch, use case or user specific naming scheme. This could be for example a reference to an electric scheme.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSMachineMonitoringConsumptionType serves as an entry point for consumption information for a WSMachine and contains information in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSMachineMonitoringHealthType provides health information for a WSMachine in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSMachineMonitoringProcessType provides process information for a WSMachine in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:MachineDesignSpeed: The design speed of the machine is the working speed of the machine. It is the theoretical production per unit of time (e.g., 1/s, kg/s, litre/s) at full capacity without interruption. It is specified separately for each product type or container and depends on parameters that depend on the product, raw materials, or semi-finished products, or on the operating conditions (e.g., product temperature).
UAWSBasis:MachineSpeed: Provides information about the machine speed and is specified in production per unit of time (e.g., 1/s, containers/h, kg/s, liters/s).
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: Provides information about the process value setpoint.

UAWSBasis:TotalProducts: The total products are the quantity of products processed in entirety. The product counters TotalProducts, GoodProducts and ScrapProducts should not be reset or must all be reset together.

UAWSBasis:GoodProducts: The good products shall be the produced quantity that meets quality requirements. The product counters TotalProducts, GoodProducts and ScrapProducts should not be reset or must all be reset together.

UAWSBasis:ScrapProducts: The scrap produts shall be the produced quantity that did not meet quality requirements and either has to be scrapped or recycled. The product counters TotalProducts, GoodProducts and ScrapProducts should not be reset or must all be reset together.

UADI:OperationCounters: This building block provides the information, how long a MachineryItem is turned on and how long it performed an activity. It uses the IOperationCounterType interface and the predefined functional group OperationCounters defined in OPC 10000-100.

UADI:PowerOnDuration: PowerOnDuration is the duration the Device has been powered. The main purpose is to determine the time in which degradation of the Device occurred. The details, when the time is counted, is implementation-specific. Companion specifications might define specific rules. Typically, when the Device has supply voltage and the main CPU is running, the time is counted. This may include any kind of sleep mode, but may not include pure Wake on LAN. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the Device and shall not be reset when the Device is restarted. The PowerOnDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

UADI:OperationDuration: OperationDuration is the duration the Device has been powered and performing an activity. This counter is intended for Devices where a distinction is made between switched on and in operation. For example, a drive might be powered on but not operating. It is not in tended for Devices always performing an activity like sensors always measuring data. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the Device and shall not be reset when the Device is restarted. The OperationDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

UAMachinery:LifetimeCounters: This building block provides the information about the past and estimated remaining lifetime of a MachineryItem or other aspects of a MachineryItem like a software license. It uses the LifetimeVariableType defined in OPC 10000-100 as base.

UAVariable.Description: The design speed of the machine is the working speed of the machine. It is the theoretical production per unit of time (e.g., 1/s, kg/s, litre/s) at full capacity without interruption. It is specified separately for each product type or container and depends on parameters that depend on the product, raw materials, or semi-finished products, or on the operating conditions (e.g., product temperature).

UAObject.Description: Provides information about the machine speed and is specified in production per unit of time (e.g., 1/s, containers/h, kg/s, liters/s).

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: Provides information about the process value setpoint.

UAVariable.Description: Provides information about the process value setpoint.
The total products are the quantity of products processed in entirety. The product counters TotalProducts, GoodProducts and ScrapProducts should not be reset or must all be reset together.
The good products shall be the produced quantity that meets quality requirements. The product counters TotalProducts, GoodProducts and ScrapProducts should not be reset or must all be reset together.

The scrap produts shall be the produced quantity that did not meet quality requirements and either has to be scrapped or recycled. The product counters TotalProducts, GoodProducts and ScrapProducts should not be reset or must all be reset together.

UAObject.Description: This building block provides the information, how long a MachineryItem is turned on and how long it performed an activity. It uses the IOperationCounterType interface and the predefined functional group OperationCounters defined in OPC 10000-100.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UADI:PowerOnDuration: PowerOnDuration is the duration the Device has been powered. The main purpose is to determine the time in which degradation of the Device occurred. The details, when the time is counted, is implementation-specific. Companion specifications might define specific rules. Typically, when the Device has supply voltage and the main CPU is running, the time is counted. This may include any kind of sleep mode, but may not include pure Wake on LAN. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the Device and shall not be reset when the Device is restarted. The PowerOnDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

UADI:OperationDuration: OperationDuration is the duration the Device has been powered and performing an activity. This counter is intended for Devices where a distinction is made between switched on and in operation. For example, a drive might be powered on but not operating. It is not in tended for Devices always performing an activity like sensors always measuring data. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the Device and shall not be reset when the Device is restarted. The OperationDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

UAVariable.Description: PowerOnDuration is the duration the Device has been powered. The main purpose is to determine the time in which degradation of the Device occurred. The details, when the time is counted, is implementation-specific. Companion specifications might define specific rules. Typically, when the Device has supply voltage and the main CPU is running, the time is counted. This may include any kind of sleep mode, but may not include pure Wake on LAN. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the Device and shall not be reset when the Device is restarted. The PowerOnDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

OperationDuration is the duration the Device has been powered and performing an activity. This counter is intended for Devices where a distinction is made between switched on and in operation. For example, a drive might be powered on but not operating. It is not in tended for Devices always performing an activity like sensors always measuring data. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the Device and shall not be reset when the Device is restarted. The OperationDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UADI:AssetId: AssetId is a user writable alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a component. The ID is provided by the integrator or user of the device. It contains typically an identifier in a branch, use case or user specific naming scheme. This could be for example a reference to an electric scheme.

UAVariable.Description: AssetId is a user writable alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a component. The ID is provided by the integrator or user of the device. It contains typically an identifier in a branch, use case or user specific naming scheme. This could be for example a reference to an electric scheme.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSUnitMonitoringConsumptionType serves as an entry point for consumption information for a WSUnit and contains information in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSUnitMonitoringHealthType provides health information for a WSUnit in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).
The WSUnitMonitoringProcessType provides process information for a WSUnit in accordance with the specifications of OPC UA for Machinery - Part 1: Basic Building Blocks (OPC 40001-1).

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:MachineDesignSpeed: The design speed of the machine is the working speed of the machine. It is the theoretical production per unit of time (e.g., pieces, volume, mass) at full capacity without interruption. It is specified separately for each product type or container and depends on parameters that depend on the product, raw materials, or semi-finished products, or on the operating conditions (e.g., product temperature).
UAWSBasis:MachineSpeed: The current machine speed is provided in the AnalogSignal node; the set machine speed is provided in the ProcessValueSetpoint node.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: Provides information about the process value setpoint.
UAWSBasis:TotalProducts: The total products are the quantity of products processed in entirety.
UAWSBasis:GoodProducts: The good products shall be the produced quantity that meets quality requirements.
UAWSBasis:ScrapProducts: The scrap produts shall be the produced quantity that did not meet quality requirements and either has to be scrapped or recycled.

UADI:OperationCounters: This building block provides the information, how long a MachineryItem is turned on and how long it performed an activity. It uses the IOperationCounterType interface and the predefined functional group OperationCounters defined in OPC 10000-100.

However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

UAWSBasis:Held: The machine is not carrying out its intended function due to an unpermitted deviation from the SET-state not detected by a control system sensor but from the operator. The operator intervenes and stops the machine manually (e.g. by handoff). If possible, the cause of failure is classed by the operator as either equipment failure or external failure.

UAPackML:Suspended: Provides information about the suspended.

UAWSBasis:LowLevelLimitSwitch: This data point is part of the WSFillLevelLimitSwitchesType and indicates whether a medium has reached and/or exceeds (true) the low level or falls below (false).

UAWSBasis:LowLowLevelLimitSwitch: This data point is part of the WSFillLevelLimitSwitchesType and indicates whether a medium has reached and/or exceeds (true) the low low level or falls below (false).

This data point is part of the WSFillLevelLimitSwitchesType and indicates whether a medium has reached and/or exceeds (true) the low level or falls below (false).

UAObjectType.Description: The WSHeaterType represents a heating device. This can be powered by electricity or fuel.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAMachinery:Monitoring: Provides information about the monitoring.
UAMachinery:Process: Provides information about the process.
UAWSBasis:IsActive: IsActive indicates whether the heater is currently in operation. For electric heaters, True means that current is flowing or that a flame is burning.

UAWSBasis:PowerLevel: The PowerLevel provides information about the output of the heater. This can be set continuously between 0 and 100%. With a binary valve position, 0% would be closed and 100% would be open.
UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: Provides information about the process value setpoint.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSTemperingDeviceType represents a temperature control device and its configuration. A temperature control device is used to control the temperature of various components, such as hoppers (WSHopperType), rollers (WSRollerType), dispensers (WSSweetsDepositor), and many more. A temperature control device can be powered electrically or with fuel.

UAObjectType.Description: The interface type IValideExternalValuesType provides the attribute IsValid. In addition to time-dependent measured values from online sensors, samples are also taken during production to determine quality parameters. These values can be determined manually in production or in a laboratory. The interface is intended for this case to mark externally determined values as valid.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:IsValid: Indicates whether the set value is valid.

UAObjectType.Description: Represents a roller component used for transporting, shaping, or processing products within a machine or component. A roller may be self or passive-driven and can optionally carry a tooling identifier.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Speed: The speed of the WSRoller can be specified in rpm or m/s, depending on the application. The unit must be set as the engineering unit.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSRollerSystemType represents a roller system consisting of one or more rollers, which can optionally be driven by one or more motors. Optional columns can be used to map the distances and contact forces between rollers or between a roller and other objects (e.g., conveyor belt). Optional TemperingDevices are used to temper the rollers.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSSteamHumidifierStationType represents a steam station for humidifying products with steam. An instance of this type is used, for example, in the sugaring station (WSSugaringStationType) during post-treatment of a Mogulan plant in the WS Sweets domain.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSProductCleaningType represents a product cleaning device, for example, to remove starch from products from a Mogul plant in the WS Sweets domain as part of post-treatment within a starch station.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSPortioningHopperType represents a hopper with integrated portioning function. The product can be removed continuously or portioned, e.g. with counter-rotating rollers. This type is used in the WS Bake domain for portioning dough, for example.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:<PortioningDevice>: The mandatory placeholder <PortioningDevice> can be used, for example, to represent the motors of two counter-rotating star rollers that shape the portions. Continuous product removal is also possible.

UAObject.Description: The mandatory placeholder <PortioningDevice> can be used, for example, to represent the motors of two counter-rotating star rollers that shape the portions. Continuous product removal is also possible.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSDepositorHopperType represents a specialized form of the WSHoperType, which is used, for example, in molding plants or mogul plants of the WS Sweets domain.

UAWSBasis:FillLevelLimitSwitches: This Object groups the fill level limit switches for different limit levels.
UAWSBasis:HighLevelLimitSwitch: This data point is part of the WSFillLevelLimitSwitchesType and indicates whether a medium has reached and/or exceeds (false) the high level or falls below (true).
UAWSBasis:LowLevelLimitSwitch: This data point is part of the WSFillLevelLimitSwitchesType and indicates whether a medium has reached and/or exceeds (true) the low level or falls below (false).

UAObjectType.Description: The WSDemouldingStationType represents the demoulding station of a moulding plant (WS Sweets domain) which removes the chocolate products from their moulds after they have set. The demoulding station usually includes mechanisms for opening the moulds, demoulding the products and transporting the finished products to downstream plants.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSChocolateTransportType represents the transport system for chocolate within a chocolate tempering machine (WSChocolateTemperType) in the WS Sweets domain.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAWSBasis:Hopper: Provides information about a hopper with optional tempering facility and optional motor. A hopper is used where masses or loose ingredients are fed (e.g. slab former, mixing, sprinkler) or collected again (shell building, scraping) in the process.

Extension.uawsbasis:ImplementationNote: UAMachineryProcessValues:ProcessValueSetpoint: This data point gives the set speed of a component and the four optional deviation variables: LowLowDeviation, LowDeviation, HighDeviation and HighHighDeviation.

UAVariable.Description: This data point gives the set speed of a component and the four optional deviation variables: LowLowDeviation, LowDeviation, HighDeviation and HighHighDeviation.

UAObjectType.Description: WSChocolateTemperType represents an integrated chocolate tempering device. For example, these components are part of the enrobing machine (WSSweetsEnrobingType) of a chocolate bar production line from the WS Sweets domain and can occur multiple times there.

UAWSBasis:ChocolateTransport: This Object is part of the chocolate temper (WSChocolateTemperType) of e.g. the enrobing of a bar production line. The WSChocolateTransportType describes the transport of the chocolate in a chocolate temper.

UAWSBasis:ChocolateTempering: This Object is part of the chocolate temper (WSChocolateTemperType) of e.g. the enrobing of a bar production line. The Chocolate Tempering describes the chocolate tempering process in a chocolate temper and provides information about the different stages of chocolate tempering.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSSprinklerType represents a sprinkler that is used to apply all types of sprinkles, such as chopped nuts or granulated sugar, to products (e.g., bars, cookies, pralines). Instances of this type are used in enrobing machines in the WS Sweets domain.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSOvenLayerType represents a baking level within a tunnel oven (WSBakeTunnelOvenMulitLayerType or WSBakeTunnelOvenSingleLayerType) from the WS Bake domain. Each level transports a product stream via its own conveyor belt and comprises one or more baking zones, which may differ in their heating principle.

UAObjectType.Description: The WSSpraySystemType represents spray systems in bakeries that apply water or oil. This can be done directly onto dough pieces using spray nozzles or by dripping oil onto the walls of a hopper, for example. Instances of the WSSpraySystemType are also used in pastry finishing with, for example, icing in a MarkUpTable.

UAObjectType.Description: Represents a component for portioning a product from a bulk input into discrete portions or into a continuous pre-band. Portioning is achieved mechanically via rollers inside the hopper and can operate in either cutting or continuous feeding mode. Typical applications include dough, marzipan, or similar processable masses.
